Q1 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider a hash table of size 10 with hash function mod 10. We want to insert the following elements into the hash table using linear probing for collision resolution: 12, 22, 32, 42, 52. After inserting all the elements, what will be the number of elements present at index 2?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
To solve this problem, we need to insert the given elements into the hash table using linear probing. Linear probing involves moving to the next slot if the current slot is already occupied.
Let’s go through the insertion process:
For element 12: h(12) = 12 % 10 = 2. Place 12 at index 2.
For element 22: h(22) = 22 % 10 = 2. Collision occurs, so place 22 at index 3.
For element 32: h(32) = 32 % 10 = 2. Collision occurs, so place 32 at index 4.
For element 42: h(42) = 42 % 10 = 2. Collision occurs, so place 42 at index 5.
For element 52: h(52) = 52 % 10 = 2. Collision occurs, so place 52 at index 6.
After inserting all the elements, the number of elements present at index 2 is 1.
Therefore, the correct answer is option (a).

Q5 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statement(s) is/are true?

Correct answer: A, C, D
Solution

(a, c, d)
Option (a) both Quick Sort and Merge Sort are both examples of divide and conquer. In Merge Sort we do blind division and try to conquer on the small sub problems while in Quick Sort we perform meaning division so we don’t bear the conquer cost.
Option (b) if we randomly choose a pivot the quick sort will not end up giving θ(n log n) time complexity in every case but less no of groups will the worst case of θ(n2), for sure.
Option (c) every partition technique takes Ο(n) to choose the pivot whether its Hoare or Lomuto analysis of Hoare vs Lomuto:https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quicksort
Option (d) given that we can find the median in Ο(1) we can always simulate Quick Sort to perform in θ(n log n) but generally the median finding takes more time and Merge Sort out performs the constant factor that gets added for median using the median of medians finding and then applying the Quick Sort.
Correct answers will be option (a), (c) and (d).

Q7 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Searching for an element in the hash table requires Ο(1) time for the ________ time, whereas for direct addressing it holds for the ________ time.

Correct answer: C, A
Solution

(c)
Searching for an element in the hash table requires Ο(1) time for the Average time, whereas for direct addressing it holds for the worst case time.

Which of the following are true?
(i) In a undirected graph G with ‘n’ vertices and ‘e’ edges, sum of degrees of vertices = 2*e.
(ii) Eccentricity of a connected graph can never be equal to radius of the graph.
(iii) Girth of a graph is the shortest cycle of the graph.
(iv) Graph with equal degree for all vertices is regular graph.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

Not attempted

A complete graph G with 5 nodes has positive weight edge, each node has distinct weight with an integer value and maximum weight is equal to the number of edges in G. What can be the maximum weight of minimum spanning tree for graph G?

NAT
Correct answer: 14
Solution

14

max weight of mst = 1+2+4+7=14
